g.text: * This IC is short on pins

g.desc: * This IC is short on pins

g.text: when the pins used for SDA

g.desc: when the pins used for SDA

g.text: and SCL are not being used

g.desc: and SCL are not being used

g.text: are used to enable the I2C

g.desc: are used to enable the I2C

g.text: * Two main buses are routed throughout the board:

g.desc: * Two main buses are routed throughout the board:

g.text: - USER: The USER bus is focused on controlling the board itself (SoC/MCU),

g.desc: - USER: The USER bus is focused on controlling the board itself (SoC/MCU),

g.text: e.g. setting up configuration, issuing commands, retrieving collected

g.desc: e.g. setting up configuration, issuing commands, retrieving collected

g.text: data, etc. This can be a multi-master bus, when cofugired as I2C. Also,

g.desc: data, etc. This can be a multi-master bus, when cofugired as I2C. Also,

g.text: as a rule, in this bus, the board (SoC/MCU) acts as a SLAVE, but depending

g.desc: as a rule, in this bus, the board (SoC/MCU) acts as a SLAVE, but depending

g.text: on the mode of operation, this bus can be used as a secondary PHER bus.

g.desc: on the mode of operation, this bus can be used as a secondary PHER bus.

g.text: - PHER: The Pheripheral bus is where the devices the board controls directly

g.desc: - PHER: The Pheripheral bus is where the devices the board controls directly

g.text: are located; e.g. the fuel gauge, battery charger, miscellaneous devices

g.desc: are located; e.g. the fuel gauge, battery charger, miscellaneous devices

g.text: like screens or EEPROMs, etc. For this bus, the board (SoC/MCU) acts

g.desc: like screens or EEPROMs, etc. For this bus, the board (SoC/MCU) acts

g.text: strictly as a MASTER. Due to the way it's routed, the PHER can never safely

g.desc: strictly as a MASTER. Due to the way it's routed, the PHER can never safely

g.text: be a multi-master bus when configured as I2C.

g.desc: be a multi-master bus when configured as I2C.

g.text: * The board operates in two distinct and fixed modes, programmable at the

g.desc: * The board operates in two distinct and fixed modes, programmable at the

g.text: - STANDALONE: The board is meant to run without supervision. It will be

g.desc: - STANDALONE: The board is meant to run without supervision. It will be

g.text: configured by default in SoC/MCU firmware, or if enabled, interactively

g.desc: configured by default in SoC/MCU firmware, or if enabled, interactively

g.text: via a pre-specified "HAT" (if supported; e.g. additional EEPROM storage,

g.desc: via a pre-specified "HAT" (if supported; e.g. additional EEPROM storage,

g.text: screen with buttons, etc.). In this mode, the board acts as MASTER in

g.desc: screen with buttons, etc.). In this mode, the board acts as MASTER in

g.text: - MANAGED: The board is meant to be controlled by a host system. The board

g.desc: - MANAGED: The board is meant to be controlled by a host system. The board

g.text: acts as a MASTER in the PHER bus, and as a SLAVE in the USER bus.

g.desc: acts as a MASTER in the PHER bus, and as a SLAVE in the USER bus.

g.text: * Both buses support specific communication modes, selectable at firmware level:

g.desc: * Both buses support specific communication modes, selectable at firmware level:

g.text: See Pinouts on the table to the side.

g.desc: See Pinouts on the table to the side.

g.text: * A secondary bus is derived from the PHER bus, the PHER_I2C (PHER_SDA and PHER_SCL).

g.desc: * A secondary bus is derived from the PHER bus, the PHER_I2C (PHER_SDA and PHER_SCL).

g.text: The lines in PHER_I2C bus are isolated by a level shifter, and enabled only

g.desc: The lines in PHER_I2C bus are isolated by a level shifter, and enabled only

g.text: when ~{PHER_EN} is deasserted (i.e. at 3.3V). I2C devices meant to be connected

g.desc: when ~{PHER_EN} is deasserted (i.e. at 3.3V). I2C devices meant to be connected

g.text: to the PHER bus MUST connect through PHER_I2C.

g.desc: to the PHER bus MUST connect through PHER_I2C.

g.text: * For the USER bus, Pull-ups are always present, and pull to VCC, which can be either

g.desc: * For the USER bus, Pull-ups are always present, and pull to VCC, which can be either

g.text: 3.3V or 5V depending on physical board configuration. However, PHER is nominally a

g.desc: 3.3V or 5V depending on physical board configuration. However, PHER is nominally a

g.text: 3.3V bus, and therefore has pull-ups to 3.3V (e.g. I2C). To the SoC/MCU all lines

g.desc: 3.3V bus, and therefore has pull-ups to 3.3V (e.g. I2C). To the SoC/MCU all lines

g.text: are 5V-tolerant, however, try to avoid presenting 5V on the PHER bus.

g.desc: are 5V-tolerant, however, try to avoid presenting 5V on the PHER bus.
